free_text: Odd-Even Differences in the Elastic Scattering of
Particles were detected in an array of
1000 Mm thick surface barrier silicon detectors.
There is  nothing else said in this article.
In the article (publ. at Phyc.Rev. C17,951,1978) of the
same authors and similar experimental conditions,
the authors gave "..the relative errors of consecutive
experimental points varied from +-0.25%, for small,
to +-20%, for large scattering angles, respectively.
They include the statistical errors and errors due to
the finite angular resolution. ..." - The experiment
was done at the same facility, at the energy range:
29-58 MeV and with 58,60Ni targets. See EXFOR D0296.
Curves from the fig.1 of the article published
from fig. 1 of ref. Phys.Rev. C11, p.628,1975
from fig. 1 of ref. Phys.Rev. C11, p.628,1975
